title: rubus rivularis subsp. horridulus
intro: Rubus rivularis subsp. horridulus, also known as the prickly raspberry or thorny bramble, is a subspecies of the Rubus genus and a type of wild raspberry native to Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of Rubus rivularis subsp. horridulus include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and boosting the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy.
